nits.finding: sinkhorn_align() returns a plan with all-NaN entries when non-convergence occurs (e.g. reg=1e-6, max_iter=10). No NaN guard post-POT call; alignment_polytope propagates silently to Vertex.joint_probability=NaN. Reproduced: reg=1e-6 → 'Numerical errors at iteration 1' → joint_probability=nan. Default reg=0.05 is unaffected (test_sinkhorn_cross_architecture_no_nan PASS). Risk is user-supplied extreme reg. Suggested fix: raise ValueError or clamp plan after ot.sinkhorn call.

top1=0.000 and top5=0.000 for ALL 7 settings. This is mathematically expected (random Gaussian decoders, feature_id_A range 0-63 mod n_feat=24 makes exact index match near-zero probability), and README:124 explicitly discloses 'synthetic alignment quality on random Gaussian decoders'. Not vacuous in the audit.yml sense, but a human reader may mistake the metric as meaningful — recommend brief inline comment in ablation script.

verdict_text: All 6 checklist items PASS. 79/79 tests green. polyalign-lint smoke clean. 36 [DEMO]-prefixed synthetic pairs. README numbers machine-sourced from ablation.json. Case C honest disclosure complete across README/CLAIM.md/NOTICE/CHANGELOG.
